ops.title: codex-ask v3 (0.20.0): Python port, run registry, dispatch guard — lifecycle invariants
ops.body:
Supersedes ad8a8cb (bash-era lifecycle doc). Plugin **0.20.0** reworked the codex dispatch stack after the 2026-07-18 "frozen lanes" incident. RCA: the on-disk protocol was SOUND (both codex runs completed with clean `status=0`); the failure was ABOVE it — two wrapper subagents backgrounded `codex-ask` as Bash tasks and idled for a completion notification that never fires for in-process subagents (harness gap, filed anthropics/claude-code#78782). Every remaining prose-only invariant (foreground-only, no-ccx-in-lanes, concurrency folklore) was a repeat of the 10-day pattern where prose rules get violated live. Fix = move invariants into mechanism; verified by an 8-item live matrix (real dispatch, real Bash-timeout+`--await` recovery, hook live-fire, `--ps` cross-invocation recovery, `--mint-root`/`--collect`, `--schema`, marker canary, MCP-off) plus the pytest battery (`-k codex`, 32 green) and 29 hook inline tests on capt-hook 10.0.0+10.1.0.

## What changed from the bash era
- **`bin/codex-ask` is now a PEP 723 uv script** (`#!/usr/bin/env -S uv run --quiet --script`, `requires-python>=3.13`, zero deps — no resolver on the dispatch path). Same CLI surface, goldened by the existing subprocess suite.
- **Fixed run registry.** Every run (ad-hoc + `--mint-root` roots) lands under `${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-~/.cache}/codex-ask/runs/` (override `CODEX_ASK_RUNS_DIR`). The filesystem IS the registry: **`codex-ask --ps`** walks it, classifies each run from disk, emits one JSONL record (state/pid/started/log_age/cwd/session/reply_file), and prunes only prefix-owned (`codex-ask.`/`codex-root.`), meta-bearing, terminal, aged (>7d) dirs — reclassified immediately before rmtree (TOCTOU-safe), containers expanded so a live lane under a minted root is never pruned. Kills the zombie class: a dead/never-woken caller's run is recoverable from ANY session, not only via one AWAIT stdout line.
- **Detach is native double-fork** (`os.setsid()` + orphan-to-PID-1 + re-exec `--worker`), replacing `set -m`+braced-group. Same timeout-survival contract; the intermediate fork pipes the worker pid back so the PARENT writes pid+lstart (closes the "meta exists, pid never appears → pending forever" window the naive port opened).
- **pid-recycle is FIXED, not deferred.** `pid_alive` = `kill -0` AND recorded `ps -o lstart=` matches, both taken under pinned `TZ=UTC LC_ALL=C /bin/ps` (a TZ/locale change — this host's `/etc/localtime` moved 2026-07-16 — must not wedge a live lane); blank/missing lstart degrades to bare kill-0 (covers legacy bash runs mid-upgrade).
- **MCP off in lanes**: `-c mcp_servers={}` on the exec line (verified live: 0 mcp refs in a real run's log) + a no-ccx/no-MCP rule in the plugin `AGENTS.md` fed as developer_instructions. Basis: 18-run A/B (note 02590cf) — zero gain, real overhead, wedge risk.
- **Dropped `-e/--effort`** (xhigh pinned). `--schema` kept and is now THE verdict-lane mechanism (prose warnings deleted).

## Preserved lifecycle invariants (still load-bearing)
- Non-empty `$R` ⇔ codex exited 0 with a complete reply (staged `.tmp`→`os.replace` only on rc==0 && non-empty, before status). Failed runs unlink `.tmp`.
- `meta` present ⇒ latest launch attempt (reuse wipe clears status/pid/lstart/meta/cmd before fresh meta; codex launches after meta). Live-lane refusal: relaunch into an `-s` dir with meta + alive pid (or meta mtime <5s) is refused ("lane busy").
- `--collect`/`--ps` are read-only, disk-only; a minted-but-never-run lane reads `no-run`. classify is race-tolerant (retries once on OSError).
- Death-by-absence: literal grep of `"type":"turn.started"`/`"turn.completed"` in codex's `--json` log, unanchored, fail-OPEN (marker canary in the live matrix guards against a codex event-schema rename silently defanging it). Signal exits normalized to 128+n.
- Print-first: `REPLY_FILE:`/`LOG_FILE:`/`AWAIT:` flush before detach, so a killed call still carries its `--await` recipe.

## Enforcement (the prose→mechanism move)
`plugins/codex/hooks/dispatch.py` ships a capt-hook pack (`hooks/capt-hook.toml` `[pack]` form, plugin.json dep `captain-hook >=10.0.0`). Two `CustomCommandLineCondition` rules (built on `cc_transcript.command` + `captain_hook.util.shell.normalize_executable`, following `DangerousCommandLine`): block backgrounded `codex-ask` (run_in_background, span-associated bare `&`, nohup/setsid/disown — executable-position only, quoted `&` never counts) and hand-rolled `codex exec` (basename-normalized, global-flag-skipped, wrapper-unwrapped, `sh -c`/`eval` recursed). Out of scope by construction (documented, not chased): screen/tmux/coproc detachers and wrapper scripts hiding the codex call. Do NOT edit captain-hook's `Runs` primitive (cross-repo semantic blast radius); unwrap inside the custom check.

## Prose is consolidated (one canon)
`skills/codex/SKILL.md` is the single canon; `agents/codex-wrapper.md` is a ~15-line relay drill (self-sufficient — wrappers don't load SKILL.md); `plugin/guides/md/claude-rules.md` (fleet fragment → CLAUDE.md via daily cron) shrank to principle + pointer; repo-bootstrap base-conventions stays a pointer.

See the RCA note for the incident timeline and the finder/refuter dispositions.
ops.when: before changing codex-ask (lifecycle, --collect/--ps classification, staged reply, detach/worker, pid liveness), the codex dispatch guard hook, or the codex skill/wrapper prose
